reasoning: 4,4'-methylenebis(N,N-dimethylcyclohexanamine) consists of two N,N-dimethylcyclohexylamine units linked by a methylene bridge; each nitrogen bears two methyl groups and one cyclohexyl group, making each amine center a dialkylamine (secondary amine with two alkyl substituents on nitrogen, where N-methyl and N-cyclohexyl count as dialkyl). The compound is an organic nitrogen compound classified as a dialkylamine.
